perturbation.original_text: "Customer" has the meaning ascribed thereto in the Preamble.
perturbation.changed_text: "Customer" has the meaning ascribed thereto in the Preamble. For the purposes of liability and regulatory compliance, Customer shall also mean the 'Designated Representative' which assumes all responsibilities and obligations outlined for the Customer in this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Customer' simply refers to the Preamble. By adding that it also means 'Designated Representative' for specific obligations, this introduces a new undefined term and potentially shifts responsibilities to an ambiguous entity. This directly contradicts sections where 'Customer' is mentioned as Profound Medical Inc.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If Customer or any relevant Governmental Authority determines that a recall campaign is necessary, Customer will implement such recall campaign at Customer's sole cost and risk. Customer hereby covenants and agrees that it shall bear all costs and expenses related to the implementation of any such recall. In case of a recall of the Product, Philips shall at its sole cost provide full cooperation to Customer in order to achieve an efficient and effective recall by Customer. Philips shall provide such cooperation at its own costs, including internal organizational costs but not including the costs of repair, replacement, installation of Products and logistics related to the recall [Redacted - Commercially Sensitive]. For greater certainty, the obligations of the Parties pursuant to this clause 7.6 shall only apply in respect of Products sold or distributed on or after the Effective Date, and for clarity, to thwe extent there is a recall that involves any Product sold or distributed prior to the Effective Date, the covenants and obligations of the Parties pursuant to this clause 7.6 shall not apply (and shall be addressed by the terms of the Purchase Agreement).
perturbation.justification: The modified text expands the definition of 'Customer' to include 'Designated Representative' for liability and regulatory compliance. The contradicted text explicitly states obligations of 'Customer', such as implementing a recall campaign at 'Customer's sole cost and risk' and cooperating with Philips in case of a recall. By including 'Designated Representative' in the definition of 'Customer' for these specific obligations, it introduces ambiguity as to who bears the cost and risk of the recall campaign. Is it Profound Medical Inc. ('Customer' as defined in the Preamble), the 'Designated Representative', or both? This directly contradicts the original intent where 'Customer' (Profound Medical Inc.) solely bears these responsibilities, leading to potential disputes and making the original clause regarding recall obligations unclear and potentially unenforceable. The addition changes a key definition used in another clause, causing a direct conflict within the document, and aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: "Product" means the Sonalleve MR-guided HIFU device which Philips shall manufacture and supply according to this Agreement as specified in Schedule 1,including spare parts.
perturbation.changed_text: "Product" means the Sonalleve MR-guided HIFU device or any 'Equivalent Device' which Philips shall manufacture and supply according to this Agreement as specified in Schedule 1,including spare parts. An 'Equivalent Device' is defined as a device performing substantially similar functions, even if not explicitly listed in Schedule 1.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Product' is extended to include an undefined 'Equivalent Device'. This could lead to disputes over whether a device qualifies as equivalent and whether Philips is obligated to supply it under the original agreement's terms. The definition makes it unclear which 'Products' Philips is actually obligated to manufacture and supply, as referenced in the general scope.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term, Philips shall manufacture and supply and Customer shall purchase the Products ordered by Customer pursuant to Purchase Orders (issued by Customer and accepted by Philips) in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces the term 'Equivalent Device' into the definition of 'Product,' without specifying what constitutes an 'Equivalent Device' in Schedule 1. This directly contradicts the original definition, which explicitly tied the 'Product' to what is specified in Schedule 1. The contradicted text states that Philips shall manufacture and supply the 'Products' ordered, creating uncertainty about whether Philips is obligated to manufacture and supply an 'Equivalent Device' if it's not explicitly listed in Schedule 1. This creates a direct conflict regarding Philips' manufacturing and supply obligations, as it's unclear which 'Products' (including or excluding Equivalent Devices) Philips must manufacture. The ambiguity in the definition of 'Product' directly undermines the clarity of the supply obligations in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: "Specifications" means the specifications for the Product as set out in Schedule 1, as such specifications be amended from time to time in accordance with the terms of the quality agreement (the "Quality Agreement").
perturbation.changed_text: "Specifications" means the specifications for the Product as set out in Schedule 1, which may be updated periodically by Philips at its discretion without requiring amendment to the Quality Agreement. Customer will be notified of any such changes to the specifications of the Product.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition ties specification changes to the Quality Agreement. The modified text allows Philips to unilaterally change specifications, creating a conflict of interest. The contradiction arises because it bypasses the Quality Agreement requirements specified in other parts of the contract. The new statement can change unilaterally the 'Product' without consulting with the other party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Philips represents and warrants to Customer that all Products delivered and any services provided hereunder: i. conform to the Specifications on the Delivery Date [Redacted - Commercially Sensitive]; ii. unless otherwise agreed with Customer in writing, are new (do not contain any used or reconditioned parts or materials) and fit for the purposes for which they are intended; iii. are of sound workmanship, good quality and free from defects in construction, manufacture and material [Redacted - Commercially Sensitive]; iv. the manufacturing and shipment of the Product comply in all respects with applicable Laws, regulations, certification requirements, including health and safety standards and all other applicable regulatory requirements for the manufacture and shipment of Products; v. are free and clear of all liens, encumbrances, and other Claims against title; and vii. comply in all respects with the terms of this Agreement and the applicable Purchase Orders.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Specifications' can only be amended in accordance with the Quality Agreement. The changed text allows Philips to unilaterally update the specifications without amending the Quality Agreement. This directly contradicts the clause that Philips warrants that all Products delivered conform to the Specifications, because now Philips can change the Specifications without Customer agreement, potentially leading to non-conforming products that were previously conforming under the original agreement. This creates a strong contradiction as the warranty relies on a fixed or mutually agreed-upon specification, which is no longer guaranteed.
